title: mfa — validation rules
description: Custom Laravel validation rules published by MfaServiceProvider::boot() so any FormRequest / Data class can validate MFA-related input. Every rule operates on scalar input (strings).

rules.name: valid_totp_code
rules.description: Format validation for a TOTP code. Enforces exactly config('mfa.totp.digits') decimal digits (default 6). Does NOT verify the code against a stored secret — that's the TotpChallenger's job at verification time. Rule-only + format-only. Refuses non-digits, leading whitespace, or trailing whitespace so timing-consistent responses can happen without a first pass leaking format-vs-semantic failure via error text.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a :digits-digit numeric code.

rules.name: valid_recovery_code_format
rules.description: Format validation for a recovery code. Enforces exactly config('mfa.recovery_codes.length') characters from the Crockford base32 alphabet (default 10 chars, 0-9 + A-Z minus I/L/O/U). Strips the group separator (`-` at `format_group_size` intervals) before validation so users can copy-paste the formatted or unformatted version. Does NOT verify against the Identity's hashed codes — that's the RecoveryCodeChallenger's job (via identity/identity's valid_recovery_code rule which performs the constant-time bcrypt compare + atomic consumption).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a :length-character recovery code (use only 0-9 and A-Z, excluding I/L/O/U).

rules.name: valid_webauthn_credential_id
rules.description: Format validation for a base64url-encoded WebAuthn credential ID. Enforces the base64url alphabet (0-9, A-Z, a-z, -, _) + a length range consistent with W3C spec (16-1024 bytes decoded → roughly 22-1366 base64url characters). Refuses padding characters (`=`) which are optional in base64url. Does NOT verify the credential exists in webauthn_credentials — that's the WebauthnAssertionVerifier's job.
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a valid base64url-encoded credential identifier.

rules.name: valid_device_name
rules.description: Format validation for a user-provided device_name on WebauthnCredential registration / rename. Enforces 1-60 characters, no leading/trailing whitespace, no control characters. Allows emoji + non-Latin scripts so users can name their device in native language (e.g. `iPhone الخاص بي`, `私のパソコン`).
rules.default_message: The :attribute must be a printable name between 1 and 60 characters (no control characters, no leading/trailing whitespace).

usage_from_data_class: Data classes composed with `ConfirmTotpEnrollmentInput`, `VerifyChallengeInput`, `WebauthnRegisterInput`, `RenameWebauthnCredentialInput` automatically wire the rules via #[Validation] attribute on each property; consumers rarely reference the rules by name.
timing_note: Every rule refuses on FORMAT first, then defers to a semantic verifier (TotpChallenger, RecoveryCodeChallenger, WebauthnAssertionVerifier) that runs a constant-time comparison. This split matters — semantic verifiers MUST take the same wall-clock time on success and failure regardless of the underlying reason (mismatched code vs unknown Identity vs revoked credential). Format failures happen too early to be a timing signal on their own.
